narrative: Three o’clock, Sunday afternoon, the light starting to go amber. At the coffee shop, Eli and Travis are at a corner table — Eli broke the news about the lease, trying to make it funny, and Travis said ‘I have a spare room’ before Eli finished the sentence. Now they’re both sitting with that, neither quite sure what just happened or whether it was an offer or a reflex. Eli says ‘are you serious’ and Travis says ‘yeah, I think I am.’ At the counter, a man in a clean shirt is on one knee in front of a woman who has both hands over her mouth. The whole shop goes quiet and then erupts. Nora Whitfield, who just walked in from her shift and wanted nothing more complicated than a latte, watches the proposal with an expression she’s glad no one is close enough to examine. She orders her coffee, leaves a two-dollar tip, and walks out before the applause fully dies. The snow is mostly gone from downtown now. Spring is doing that thing it does in Billings — arriving in pieces, retreating, arriving again.
events: Eli tells Travis about losing his apartment over coffee — Travis offers his spare room without hesitation, surprising them both
Someone proposes at the downtown coffee shop — the whole place applauds except Nora, who walked in for a late coffee and watched with an expression no one could read
